what_it_does: Documents honest limitations and gated aspects of using Obsidian to view a typed-address ledger, specifically graph fidelity, title readability, and mobile-sync dependencies.
observations: Lists three lossy or gated aspects of the current setup
Contrasts Obsidian's path-based wikilink graph with typed, address-based edges such as board://, image://, code://, and decision://
Claims roughly 50 edges would not appear in Obsidian's graph view
Notes that item IDs like item-xxxx render Obsidian surfaces unreadable without a human-title pass
Flags iOS sync as dependent on the WSL vs Windows sync infrastructure
standouts: Explicit admission that Obsidian cannot faithfully render typed cross-scheme edges
Quantified claim that about 50 edges would be invisible in Obsidian
Honest, caveat-first framing rather than a feature description
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Includes a history array recording the filed transition
Prefixes the title with OB3 and an honest qualifier
Presents content as a bullet list under a single H1 heading
concerns: The ~50 missing-edge figure is asserted without derivation in this file
The iOS + SYNC note is vague and references an unconfirmed infrastructure choice
Uses words like 'canonical' and 'real' as value judgments without defining them locally
notes: A candid limitations note warning that Obsidian views are incomplete and need human-readable titles before use.
questions: How was the ~50 missing-edge count derived?
Which specific sync setup is intended for iOS access?
What does OB3 denote in this thread?
What is the nature of the linked item-156ec226?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes: the title and content both enumerate lossy or gated aspects of the system.
apparent_themes: Typed-link fidelity versus generic graph visualization
Readability of machine-generated identifiers for humans
intention_signals: Set expectations that Obsidian is a secondary, lossy view and that the typed ledger remains the authoritative source of relationships, while flagging usability and sync blockers.
novelty: Explicitly quantifies graph-view data loss and frames the ledger's typed edges as non-portable into a mainstream note-taking graph.
contribution: Records known fidelity, readability, and deployment caveats that any fused viewer or migration must account for.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item documenting honest limitations of Obsidian as a view over a typed-address ledger, covering graph fidelity loss, unreadable item IDs, and iOS sync dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: To warn consumers that Obsidian graph and titles are incomplete or gated representations of the underlying typed ledger.
